name: node-imurmurhash
title: node-imurmurhash
summary: incremental implementation of MurmurHash3 hashing algorithm
description:
incremental implementation of MurmurHash3 hashing algorithm
This module is an incremental implementation of the MurmurHash3 (32-bit)
hashing algorithm for JavaScript based on Gary Court's implementation with
kazuyukitanimura's modifications.
.
This version works significantly faster than the non-incremental version if
you need to hash many small strings into a single hash, since string
concatenation (to build the single string to pass the non-incremental version)
is fairly costly. In one case tested, using the incremental version was about
50% faster than concatenating 5-10 strings and then hashing.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-imurmurhash because incremental implementation of MurmurHash3 hashing algorithm.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-imurmurhash as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-imurmurhash with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-imurmurhash --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
